commit.message:
Septons CRE signal recorder workflow

Cron-triggered CRE workflow that fetches AI signals from the Septons API,
reaches DON consensus on confidence/platform/direction, and writes
signed reports to SignalRegistry.sol on Ethereum Sepolia.

Anchor tower to bottom so words build up from foundation

justify-end pb-8: single word sits at screen bottom, subsequent
words stack upward — visually builds from the ground up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Dynamic rise distance: each row rises from proportional depth below base

Bottom row rises 40px, top row rises (N-1)*50+40px — words physically
travel from the foundation level up to their final position.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Increase towerRowIn translateY for more dramatic rise from base

60px travel distance makes each row visibly rise up from below
instead of barely-noticeable 20px nudge.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix word placement to build tower from base

Iterate placement loop bottom→top so base rows (wide, appear first in
animation) win ties over observation deck rows. Big/prominent words now
land at the foundation and visibly appear first as the tower builds up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Make tower build animation visibly bottom-to-top

Increase row stagger from 40ms to 100ms and reduce per-row duration
from 500ms to 400ms, making the cascading bottom-to-top build effect
clearly perceivable (~1.1s total for 8 rows).

Fix confidence and predicted margin calculations

labels more intuitive. Predicted margin is now derived from rounded
scores so the displayed margin always matches the score difference.

fix: use layer.enabled for true circular avatar clipping in SDDM

QML clip:true only clips to the rectangular bounding box, NOT to
rounded corners. Replace with layer.enabled which renders the
Rectangle+children to an FBO then paints using the rounded shape,
producing a true circular mask. Border ring drawn as separate
element on top of the layer.

Replace YouTube Search API with RSS feed for live detection

The Search API costs 100 quota units per call, burning through the
10,000 daily limit in hours. The new approach uses the free RSS feed
to get recent video IDs, then the Videos API (1 unit) to check live
status — a 99% quota reduction.

Also removes temporary debug endpoint.

commit.message:
fix(oauth): URL-encode callback_url query parameter (#2201)

The OAuth callback URL (http://localhost:PORT/callback) was interpolated
directly into the auth URL query string without encoding. The colons and
slashes could cause ambiguous parsing on strict URL parsers or proxies,
potentially breaking the OAuth flow. Other parameters in the same URL
(spawn_agent, spawn_cloud) were already correctly encoded.

commit.message:
feat(genui): introduce Generative Interface Runtime (#19099)

This PR implements the initial **Generative Interface Runtime (GenUI)**,
enabling agents to dynamically generate structured user interfaces.

**Additions:**
- **GenUI Schema & Typings:** Defined `GenerativeInterface` and
`UIBlock` interfaces.
- **Runtime Components:** Added `InterfaceBuilder` for constructing UI
and `UIDiffEngine` for interface mutation over turns.
- **Context Persistence:** Implemented `InterfaceContext` for
session-based UI state memory.
- **Agent Output Contract:** Extended agent outputs (`AgentOutput`) to
optionally include `GenerativeInterface`.
- **Governance & Standards:** Created missing `.md` rules for data
handling, operational runbooks, standard-mapping (e.g. MCP-UI), and repo
assumptions.
- **Monitoring & CI Guards:** Initialized `genui-drift.ts`,
`genui-latency.ts` and GitHub workflows for schema checks and scheduled
drift detection.

**Pre-commit Checks:**
- Code successfully passes isolated typechecks (`tsc --noEmit`).
- No conflicting state or pre-existing dependencies touched
unnecessarily.

---

v2.6.10: Add attractor sound to mini-hordes for reliable convergence

pathToSound + setTarget alone not enough in B42 to drive zombie movement.
The attractor sound (getWorldSoundManager:addSound) creates an actual
game-world noise event that triggers the zombie hearing AI - this is
what makes siege zombies reliably converge on players.

Added attractor sound at spawn and every 5 seconds on repath, matching
siege behavior exactly. Mini-horde zombies should now hunt like siege.

commit.message:
fix: Pillars themeroom loop bounds off-by-one causing 3 mineralize misses

Lua `for x = 0, (rm.width / 4) - 1 do` with rm.width=10 runs x=0,1
(since 1.5 is the upper bound). The JS translation `x < (rm.width / 4)`
evaluated to `x < 2.5`, running x=0,1,2 — three iterations instead of two.

The extra x=2 iteration placed HWALL terrain at absolute (13,10), (14,10),
(13,15), (14,15), (13,18), (14,18) — one tile past room 0's right wall.
This converted 3 STONE tiles (at x=14) to HWALL, reducing the mineralize-
eligible tile count at depth=1 from 590 (C) to 587 (JS), causing RNG
divergence at normalized index 6210 (step 1) for seed=331 and similar seeds.

Fix: use Math.floor(rm.width / 4) and Math.floor(rm.height / 4) in the
Pillars loop to match Lua's floor semantics for non-multiple-of-4 widths.

Result: seed331 first RNG divergence moves from step 1 to step 222.
Golden typ grids updated for seeds 1, 7, 100, 555, 2468 (all had Pillars
rooms at depth=5/3 with 10-wide rooms).

Closes/advances issue #214 (agent:ux). Lore updated in docs/LORE.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add @person.name assignee support to kanban tasks and todos

Extends parser/serializer to handle optional @assignee notation at end
of kanban task and todo lines. Adds assignee field to API routes, purple
badge UI with inline editing in KanbanCard and Todos components, and
assignee input to add-task/add-todo forms. Includes test coverage for
assignee parsing and round-trip fidelity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix missing address data across all order/delivery processes

Branch postalCode, address, and managerPhone are now fetched as
fallbacks in every API endpoint that returns or saves order items:
- GET /api/order (list) - Branch address fallback for savedItems
- GET /api/order/[id] (detail/PDF) - Branch address fallback
- POST /api/order (create) - Enrich items with Branch address before saving to step1Notes
- PUT /api/order/[id] (update) - Enrich items with Branch address before saving
- POST /api/delivery-process/auto-create - Branch address fallback for delivery creation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: implement Phase 3 k8s deployment — manifests, pool controller, server split

Three-layer k8s architecture: host pods (HTTP ingress), agent-runtime pods
(session dispatch via NATS), sandbox pods (warm pool with gVisor isolation).

- K8s manifests: namespace, deployments, NATS cluster, network policies,
  Cloud SQL proxy, sandbox pod templates (light/heavy tiers)
- Pool controller: reconciliation loop, warm pod scaling, Prometheus metrics
- Workspace provisioning: GCS cache → git clone → empty dir fallback
- Server refactor: host-process.ts (HTTP→NATS bridge) and
  agent-runtime-process.ts (NATS→processCompletion) for k8s deployment
- NATS session protocol: shared types, encode/decode, subject helpers
- NATS bridge + LLM proxy: credential-free claude-code in sandbox pods
- Fix nats-sandbox-dispatch.ts this-binding bug in object literal

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

🎨 Palette: Add ARIA labels to CustomizableWidget icon buttons (#19096)

💡 **What**: Added `aria-label` attributes to the Maximize/Minimize and
Close icon-only buttons in `CustomizableWidget.tsx`.
🎯 **Why**: Icon-only buttons without `aria-label` attributes are read as
blank by screen readers, making them inaccessible to visually impaired
users who rely on these tools. Adding labels clearly communicates the
purpose of these actions.
📸 **Before/After**: N/A (Visuals are unchanged, but semantic meaning in
HTML is improved)
♿ **Accessibility**: Enhanced screen-reader support by ensuring
interactive buttons have accessible text labels (`Minimize widget`,
`Maximize widget`, `Close widget`).

AGENT-METADATA
## Assumption Ledger
- The buttons `Minimize2`, `Maximize2`, and `X` from `lucide-react`
provide no inherent text for screen readers when wrapped in the `Button`
component, requiring explicit `aria-label` overrides.

## Diff Budget
- Minimal additions (+2 localized properties).

## Success Criteria
- The `<Button>` components in
`apps/web/src/features/dashboard/widgets/CustomizableWidget.tsx` possess
valid and context-accurate `aria-label` attributes.
- No existing tests or lints broken by the change.

## Evidence Summary
- Successfully compiled and verified through manual static analysis and
Playwright verification scripts.

(both API calls fail or return unparseable data), now falls back to
deriving signals from analyzeSentimentFromResponses posWords/negWords
and extracting basic quotes directly from raw response text.

so a single emission failure cannot prevent subsequent emissions.

so the playbook sectionReady flag can resolve during progressive
rendering. All sectionReady flags are still unconditionally set to
true on both success and error completion paths.

commit.message:
🛡️ Sentinel: [CRITICAL] Fix hardcoded signing secrets in export endpoints (#19095)

🚨 **Severity:** CRITICAL
💡 **Vulnerability:** The `EXPORT_SIGNING_SECRET` used to sign and verify
export manifests was falling back to a hardcoded string (`'dev-secret'`)
when the environment variable was missing.
🎯 **Impact:** If a production environment was misconfigured to omit this
secret, the system would silently fall back to `'dev-secret'`,
potentially allowing attackers to forge valid signatures, bypass
verification checks, and compromise provenance and data integrity.
🔧 **Fix:** The fallback logic was removed. The endpoints now correctly
throw an error or return a 500 status code
(`server_configuration_error`) when the secret is missing, adhering to
the "fail securely" principle.
✅ **Verification:** Verified by running `pnpm run build:server` and
confirming no new errors were introduced in the modified files. Also
reviewed code logic to ensure a secure failure path.

This PR adheres to Sentinel's < 50-line security fix policy.

---

isBscChainName, resolvePortfolioChainKey, formatRouteAddress,
getTokenExplorerUrl (all chains + validation), getRecentTradeGroupKey,
loadRecentTrades/persistRecentTrades, fetchBscTokenMetadata
(validation, happy path, error handling), and exported constants.

* refactor: split CompanionView.tsx into header + state hook

Extract CompanionHeader (486 lines) and useCompanionViewState (155 lines)
from CompanionView.tsx, reducing it from 712 to 279 lines.

* refactor: split VrmEngine.ts into focused modules

Extract VrmBlinkController, VrmAnimationLoader, VrmFootShadow, and
VrmCameraManager from VrmEngine.ts, reducing it from 1064 to 498 lines.

* docs: add changelog entry for companion UI PR

* refactor: remove out-of-scope features from companion UI PR

Remove Pi AI (#814), telegram-enhanced (#815), hooks tests (#813),
and upload-limit fix (#816) from this branch. These features will
be submitted as separate PRs against develop.

* refactor: extract wallet trading panel to separate PR

Remove CompanionWalletPanel, WalletSwapPanel, WalletSendPanel,
WalletTradeHistory, WalletPortfolioList, WalletNftGallery,
WalletTradingProfileModal, and associated hooks from companion
UI branch. Wallet address display retained as placeholder pill.

Financial transaction code requires dedicated security review
in its own PR with full diff visibility.

* fix: validate Xvfb resolution before checking active display

Move resolution format validation before the xdpyinfo display check in
ensureXvfb(). Previously, if Xvfb was already running on the requested
display, the function returned true without validating the resolution,
causing the "invalid resolution" test to fail on Linux CI.

* refactor: extract CompanionShell overlay from App.tsx

Move the companion shell overlay logic (~500 lines) into a dedicated
CompanionShell component. App.tsx drops from ~1000 to ~500 LOC.
The UiShellMode config ("companion" | "native") determines which
shell renders — no behavioral changes.

* chore: ignore generated .d.ts files in src/plugins/

* refactor: split CompanionShell into styles, decorations, and shell

Break the 602-line CompanionShell.tsx into three focused files:
- companion-shell-styles.ts (294) — tab flags, layout/accent helpers
- CompanionShellDecorations.tsx (179) — decorative elements, close button
- CompanionShell.tsx (158) — main component, view routing

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: orchestrator running detection + Chrome memory leak

- dashboard/server.js: fix false 'down' status — running is now true if a
  loop tick appeared within the last 5 minutes (not just startup line)
- nexus-orchestrator.js: restart Chrome every 30 loops (~30 min) to prevent
  the ~7GB memory accumulation from headless WebGL renderer

commit.message:
Redesign homepage with new design language and wire up Sanity CMS

Introduce an immersive, photography-forward homepage inspired by the bikeraft.com
design review. New design tokens (canyon, sand, river colors; H2/subheading type
scale), 4 new components (Hero, TrustBar, RiverCard, TestimonialBlock), and a
7-section homepage layout. Add homepage Sanity singleton, featured trips query,
and Studio singleton structure. Homepage now fetches rivers, featured trips, and
editorial content from Sanity with hardcoded fallbacks for bikeraft.com images.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
